summary: American Realist painter from Chadds Ford, Pennsylvania. Known for Christina's World (paralyzed girl in pink dress crawling toward Olson House in Cushing, Maine), the Helga Pictures (247 portraits of neighbor Helga Testorf), and Winter 1946. Son of illustrator N.C. Wyeth, father of Jamie Wyeth.

works.name: Christina's World (1948)
works.description: Paralyzed woman in pink dress crawling through grass toward the Olson House in Cushing, Maine. Christina Olson had Charcot-Marie-Tooth disease (often cited as polio). Wyeth's wife Betsy modeled for the figure. Farnsworth Art Museum operates the house. Hangs at the top of the escalator at MoMA. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Paralyzed/polio-crippled woman in pink dress crawling in grass toward a farmhouse (Olson House) in Cushing, Maine
examples: a woman in a pink dress crawls in the direction of the Olson House
The Farnsworth Art Museum operates a wooden house in Cushing, Maine that appears in the background

clues.clue: Wife Betsy James modeled the upper body (30 years younger than Christina); Christina had Charcot-Marie-Tooth disease (often misidentified as polio)
examples: Despite being 30 years older than the intended subject, the artist's wife Betsy James modeled the upper body of this painting's central figure
The artist of this painting replaced his original muse for the work with his wife, who was 30 years the muse's junior

clues.clue: Wyeth was buried at the painting's real-life setting; he met Christina while picking blueberries
examples: The artist of this work met its model while picking blueberries, and he chose to be buried in this painting's real-life setting

examples: Richard Prince displayed this work next to a picture of his girlfriend playing dead in a location similar to this painting's setting

clues.clue: Inspired Terence Malick's Days of Heaven
examples: This director's Days of Heaven was inspired by Andrew Wyeth's Christina's World and other pastoral paintings

examples: The painting Maga's Daughter depicts this artist's wife Betsy, who modeled in place of a woman with Charcot-Marie-Tooth disease

examples: A painting by this artist inspired Bernard Langlais's wooden sculpture of a woman with logs for arms titled Local Girl

works.name: Helga Pictures (1971-1985)
works.description: 247 portraits of German neighbor Helga Testorf from Chadds Ford, many nude, kept secret from wife Betsy. Purchased by Leonard Andrews. Primarily painted in the loft of a millhouse in Chadds Ford. Revealed in Time ('Stunning Secret') and Newsweek cover stories in 1987. Individual works include Braids, Lovers, Overflow, Black Velvet, The Prussian, Letting Her Hair Down, In the Orchard, Gone, Sheepskin, Pageboy, On Her Knees.

clues.clue: 247+ portraits of German immigrant neighbor Helga Testorf, many nude; kept secret from wife Betsy; purchased by Leonard Andrews
examples: This artist created 247 portraits of a German immigrant from Chadd's Ford with long blonde braids named Helga Testorf
Time ran a cover story on this artist's series of paintings of his neighbor Helga Testorf

clues.clue: Lovers: nude blonde-braided woman on a stool by open window, illuminated by beams of light

A nude with blonde braids sits next to an open window in this artist's Lovers

clues.clue: Braids: solemn woman in gray turtleneck wearing her hair in braids

clues.clue: Other individual works: Letting Her Hair Down, Gone (last chronological depiction), Sheepskin, Pageboy, On Her Knees, Overflow
examples: Gone was the last chronological depiction of this figure, who appeared in Sheepskin and Pageboy

works.name: Winter 1946
works.description: Boy in earflaps/oversized military wear running down Kuerner's Hill after the artist's father N.C. Wyeth was killed in a train accident at that spot. Composition similar to Christina's World. Set at Kuerner Farm in Chadds Ford.

clues.clue: Boy running down Kuerner's Hill; first painting since illustrator father was killed in train/car accident at that site
examples: A boy in earflaps runs down Kuerner's Hill in this artist's Winter 1946, the first painting since his illustrator father was killed
This artist's work entered a melancholy period after a train accident killed his father

clues.clue: Composition similar to Christina's World (figure on a hillside)
examples: The composition of the aforementioned painting, Winter, 1946, is similar to another Andrew Wyeth painting titled for this woman's 'World'

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Son of N.C. Wyeth (illustrator of Treasure Island and Rip van Winkle for Scribner's; received Howard Pyle's shoes). Father of Jamie Wyeth (Portrait of Pig, pumpkinhead self-portrait, JFK portrait). Egg-tempera technique from brother-in-law Peter Hurd. Based in Chadds Ford, PA and Cushing, ME. Brandywine Valley. Used Kuerner Farm as frequent setting. Other works: Snow Hill, The Master Bedroom, Barracoon, Breakup, Chambered Nautilus, Trodden Weed.

clues.clue: Son of N.C. Wyeth (illustrator of Treasure Island and Rip van Winkle for Scribner's); father of Jamie Wyeth (Portrait of Pig, pumpkinhead self-portrait, JFK portrait)
examples: This artist of Winter 1946 and The Master Bedroom was the father of a painter who did a controversial posthumous portrait of John F. Kennedy; he was also the son of an artist who illustrated Treasure Island for Scribner's Classics
The massive painting Portrait of Pig is by an artist with this surname, who painted a striking self-portrait of himself with a pumpkin for a head

clues.clue: Based in Chadds Ford, Pennsylvania; Brandywine Valley scenes; Kuerner Farm as frequent setting
examples: This artist, who often depicted his birthplace of Chadds Ford, Pennsylvania
Wyeth used this location in Chadd's Ford as the setting for several portraits of Helga Testorf

clues.clue: Egg-tempera technique from brother-in-law Peter Hurd; used for Christina's World
examples: This artist's brother-in-law Peter Hurd taught him the egg-tempera technique
Paul Cadmus and Jacob Lawrence contributed to a 20th-century revival of this technique, which Andrew Wyeth learned from Peter Hurd and used for Christina's World

clues.clue: Trodden Weed: self-portrait wearing Howard Pyle's shoes after an operation
examples: Another painting set in this location shows its artist wearing Howard Pyle's shoes after an operation, Trodden Weed

clues.clue: Barracoon: depicted a naked Black woman; actually painted white model (Helga) as Black
examples: In one painting, this artist depicted a white model as black and claimed the model was his family's maid Betty Hammond

clues.clue: Breakup: painting of his own severed hands on a block of ice
examples: Bronze casts of this artist's hands were recently auctioned along with the painting they inspired; that painting depicts the hands emerging from an ice floe

clues.clue: Influenced Chinese 'rustic realism'; He Duoling's Spring Breeze is Awake modeled on Christina's World
examples: Andrew Wyeth influenced an artist who was inspired by this historical event to depict a girl sitting in the grass in the painting Spring Breeze is Awake

comprehensive_summary:
Andrew Wyeth (1917-2009) was an American Realist painter based in Chadds Ford, Pennsylvania and Cushing, Maine, the son of illustrator N.C. Wyeth (who illustrated Treasure Island and Rip van Winkle for Scribner's) and father of painter Jamie Wyeth (Portrait of Pig, pumpkinhead self-portrait, posthumous JFK portrait). He learned the egg-tempera technique from his brother-in-law Peter Hurd.

Christina's World (1948) is his most iconic painting, depicting a paralyzed woman (Christina Olson, who had Charcot-Marie-Tooth disease, often misidentified as polio) in a pink dress crawling through grass toward the Olson House in Cushing, Maine. Wyeth's wife Betsy James, 30 years younger than Christina, modeled the upper body. The Farnsworth Art Museum now operates the Olson House, and the painting hangs at the top of the escalator at MoMA. Wyeth met Christina while picking blueberries and chose to be buried at the painting's real-life setting. He described it as depicting 'an extraordinary conquest of a life which most people would consider hopeless.' Related works include Wind from the Sea (fluttering curtains in Christina's house), Kitchen at Olson's, and Maga's Daughter (Betsy modeling for Christina). After Christina and her brother Alvaro died, Wyeth painted brown and blue doors representing them. The painting inspired Terence Malick's Days of Heaven, Bernard Langlais's sculpture Local Girl, and was reimagined by Kennardphillipps for a 2015 Greenpeace campaign. It also influenced Chinese 'rustic realism,' including He Duoling's Spring Breeze is Awake.

The Helga Pictures (1971-1985) comprise 247+ portraits of his German neighbor Helga Testorf, many nude, created secretly over 14 years primarily in the loft of a millhouse in Chadds Ford. They were purchased by Leonard Andrews and became a sensation when revealed on the covers of both Time ('Stunning Secret,' 1987) and Newsweek. Individual works include Lovers (nude woman on stool by open window), Braids (solemn woman in gray turtleneck), Overflow, Black Velvet, The Prussian, Letting Her Hair Down, In the Orchard, Gone (last chronological depiction), Sheepskin, Pageboy, and On Her Knees. Helga is often depicted with a medallion on a black band around her neck. She originally worked as a nurse for Karl Kuerner, and Michael Palin (of Monty Python) was the first to interview her. The catalog was the first art catalog selected by the Book-of-the-Month Club and had a VHS narrated by Charlton Heston.

Winter 1946 depicts a boy running down Kuerner's Hill, the first painting after his father N.C. Wyeth was killed in a car/train accident at that spot. Its composition is similar to Christina's World. The Kuerner Farm in Chadds Ford served as the setting for many of his works. Other notable paintings include The Master Bedroom, Trodden Weed (self-portrait wearing Howard Pyle's shoes after an operation), Snow Hill, Barracoon (painted Helga as a Black woman), Breakup (his own hands emerging from an ice floe), and Chambered Nautilus.
